audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar lampstand and Levite cleansing text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for lampstand lighting, Levite cleansing, wave-presentation, firstborn substitution, service ages, and withdrawal from heavy service. Anti-ageism, anti-shame, and service-without-superiority safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to Aaron and say to him: When you make lamps go up, toward face/front of lampstand seven lamps shall give light.
Aaron did so; toward face/front of lampstand he made its lamps give light, as YHWH commanded Moses.
This was workmanship of lampstand: hammered work of gold, from its base to its blossom, hammered work; according to pattern YHWH showed Moses, so he made lampstand.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Take Levites from midst of children of Israel and cleanse them.
Thus you shall do to them to cleanse them: sprinkle purification water upon them; let them pass razor over all their flesh, wash their garments, and cleanse themselves.
They shall take one young bull with its grain offering, fine flour mixed with oil; and a second young bull you shall take for sin/purification offering.
You shall bring Levites before tent of meeting and assemble whole congregation of children of Israel.
You shall bring Levites before YHWH, and children of Israel shall lay their hands upon Levites.
Aaron shall wave/present Levites as wave offering before YHWH from children of Israel, and they shall be for serving service of YHWH.
Levites shall lay their hands upon heads of bulls; make one as sin/purification offering and one as burnt offering to YHWH, to make atonement/covering for Levites.
You shall stand Levites before Aaron and his sons and wave/present them as wave offering to YHWH.
You shall separate Levites from midst of children of Israel, and Levites shall be mine.
Afterward Levites shall enter to serve tent of meeting; you shall cleanse them and wave/present them as wave offering.
For given, given they are to me from midst of children of Israel; in place of every one opening womb, every firstborn from children of Israel, I have taken them for myself.
For mine is every firstborn among children of Israel, among human and among beast; on day I struck every firstborn in land of Egypt I sanctified them for myself.
I took Levites in place of every firstborn among children of Israel.

I gave Levites as given ones to Aaron and his sons from midst of children of Israel, to serve service of children of Israel in tent of meeting and to make atonement/covering for children of Israel, so no plague shall be among children of Israel when children of Israel draw near to holy place.
Moses and Aaron and all congregation of children of Israel did to Levites according to all YHWH commanded Moses concerning Levites; so children of Israel did to them.
Levites purified themselves from sin/misalignment and washed their garments; Aaron waved/presented them as wave offering before YHWH, and Aaron made atonement/covering for them to cleanse them.
Afterward Levites entered to serve their service in tent of meeting before Aaron and his sons; as YHWH commanded Moses concerning Levites, so they did to them.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
This is what belongs to Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward he shall enter to serve service in work of tent of meeting.
From fifty years old he shall return from host of service and shall serve no longer.
He may minister with his brothers in tent of meeting to keep charge, but work/burden he shall not do. Thus you shall do to Levites concerning their charges.

translation_notes: Lampstand light faces inward/frontward, emphasizing ordered illumination before service begins.
Levites are presented by the whole congregation, showing communal participation in their vocation.
Firstborn substitution continues Numbers 3 and is ritual representation, not a hierarchy of human value.
The age boundary concerns physically demanding tabernacle work. At fifty, Levites are not discarded: they continue assisting and keeping charge without heavy burden.
Cleansing language is ritual preparation, not body shame or moral disgust.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 8 to rank clergy above others, shame bodies, or treat older workers as useless. The chapter distinguishes heavy labor from continued experienced service and protects the community through shared, accountable vocation.
